title: Microsoft restructures its Xbox division with mass layoffs and studio closures
summary: The company cuts 3,200 jobs, sells studios, and acknowledges the failure of its subscription strategy, while evaluating changes in hardware.

title: Anthropic launches its Claude Cowork agent on mobile devices and web
summary: The AI assistant is now available on smartphones and browsers to perform tasks autonomously.

title: Sony eliminates physical games on PlayStation triggering a wave of criticism
summary: The decision to stop producing physical media for new titles triggers a petition with nearly 200,000 signatures and thousands of complaints on social media.

title: Samsung breaks profit records in its chip division and becomes the most profitable company in the world
summary: Samsung's semiconductor division records profits exceeding those of the last 40 years combined, driven by memory demand.

title: Valve fixes the red line issue on Steam Machine by simply clearing the CMOS
summary: The dreaded red line of death on Steam Machines was not a GPU failure and has a simple official remedy: clearing the CMOS memory.

title: Intel Nova Lake processors will regain AVX-512 compatibility
summary: The upcoming Intel desktop chips will again include AVX-512 instructions in their performance and efficiency cores according to Linux patches.

title: UK grants national importance status to data centers to accelerate their construction
summary: The British government allows data center projects to bypass local regulations and reduce timelines, saving billions in disputes.

title: Critical backdoor discovered in cheap Tenda routers
summary: The CERT/CC agency warns that Tenda routers contain a hidden administrator account that allows unauthorized remote access.

title: Elon Musk's xAI merges with SpaceX and renames to SpaceXAI
summary: Musk's two companies join into a single entity called SpaceXAI, in a move that seeks to simplify corporate structure and attract Wall Street.

title: DeepSeek expands beyond AI models and develops its own chip to compete with NVIDIA
summary: The Chinese artificial intelligence company announces plans to design its own chips and stop relying on external suppliers.

title: Microsoft 365 Copilot reduces costs and faces low adoption among users
summary: Microsoft removes OpenAI and Anthropic models to lower the cost, while the weekly usage rate of the assistant does not reach 1%.

title: X launches a video editor for iOS with subtitles and green screen
summary: The social network introduces editing tools to encourage original content creation and reduce stolen posts.

title: Anthropic discovers a hidden workspace in Claude and makes its internal monologue readable
summary: Through the new Jacobian Lens, researchers can read Claude's internal reasoning and reveal that the model develops a hidden J-space.

title: Longsys, Lexar parent company, surges profits 60,000% due to memory demand
summary: The Chinese memory and storage chip manufacturer expects profits of nearly 1.5 billion dollars in the first half, while preparing new DDR5 RGB modules.

title: US Supreme Court requires warrant to access mobile location data
summary: The court rules that police need a warrant to collect geolocation data from phones, reinforcing digital privacy.

title: FBI and Google dismantle a botnet that infected 2 million Smart TVs
summary: The joint operation eliminated a network of hijacked devices used for large-scale cyber attacks.

title: Meta data center contaminates city water supply with lethal bacteria
summary: Authorities investigate the leakage of dangerous bacteria from Meta's artificial intelligence facilities into the municipal water network.

title: FCC eliminates rule requiring ISPs to list all their fees
summary: The decision revokes a Biden-era policy intended to make internet costs transparent for consumers.

title: Half-Life 2 now playable in the browser at over 100 FPS
summary: An ingenious port recreates the entire game campaign using WebGL 2, with support for saved games and console.

title: Pentagon launches 'War Force' campaign to attract software engineers
summary: The US Department of Defense seeks to recruit tech talent through an initiative that promises to work on advanced defense projects.
